divider: *(Teacher language moving from §1.7 lesson → §1.8 Exit Check. No student action during the frame.)*
tutor_narration: "You just wrote the same fact two ways. Now I'm going to show you a different rectangle and a different fact. Show me you can do the same thing — two ways around — with a fact you haven't seen yet today. Three quick questions. Take your time."
divider_2: *(The frame deliberately names "a different rectangle" and "a different fact" so the student is oriented to the transfer task. Required phrase "two ways around" carries forward from the lesson. Balance-scale framing is not re-invoked here unless Heavy-tier remediation triggers post-EC.)*

purpose: Test the reversed-orientation Focus skill bidirectionally. The L.3 operations-on-both-sides recognition exposure is NOT exit-checked — EC.1 tests only reversed-orientation recognition and production.
toy: §2.10 Drop Down (production sub-items EC2, EC3) + §2.11 Multiple Choice / Checkbox (recognition sub-item EC1) + Hundred Grid Mode B/C companion display.
introduction: "Now show what you know. Three quick questions about the same fact, both ways around."

ec1.display: `5 × 8 = 40` rectangle displayed (canonical-adjacent fact, NOT the 4 × 5 instructional rectangle). §2.11 Checkbox. Candidates: `5 × 8 = 40` ✓, `40 = 5 × 8` ✓, `5 + 8 = 40` ✗, `8 × 40 = 5` ✗.
ec1.skill_tested: Recognition: both reversed orientations, both must be selected
ec1.mastery_contribution: Strong requires both correct selected with no distractors

ec2.display: `7 × 6 = 42` displayed as given. §2.10 Drop Down stem: `The same rectangle shows ___ × ___ = ___ AND ___ = ___ × ___ because the equal sign means both sides have the same value.`
ec2.student_task: Complete the stem — fill both orientations.
ec2.skill_tested: Production: reversed form from given standard
ec2.mastery_contribution: Needs Practice if EC1+EC3 correct but EC2 wrong

ec3.display: `36 = 9 × 4` displayed as given. §2.10 Drop Down stem: `The same rectangle shows ___ × ___ = ___ AND ___ = ___ × ___ because the equal sign means both sides have the same value.`
ec3.student_task: Complete the stem — fill both orientations.
ec3.skill_tested: Production: standard form from given reversed
ec3.mastery_contribution: Still Gathering if 0–1 items correct OR if EC1 selects only `a × b = c` and rejects `c = a × b`

vocabulary_check: No "factor," "factor pair," "prime," "composite," "height," "handle," bare "squares," or WODB language. "Multiple" / "is a multiple of" is permitted in EC items (Abstract section). No operations-both-sides items in EC.1.
required_phrase: `The same rectangle shows ___ × ___ = ___ AND ___ = ___ × ___ because the equal sign means both sides have the same value.`
